nodes.body: This should be enough to identify all clones happening within Repoman execution. There is another codepath for cloning but looks like that is for templates (and already logs when it's cloning (see GitProvider.scala, log "Cloning template repository...")
Yeah, that's my code. Sending it back to the RepositoryStrategy would've been a pain, so I just did the cloning there. Might change it if I have time / find an easy way.
No need to change it, was just explaining why I added the log here and not in other places. thanks for clarifying!

This is kind of a critical problem with this method. If new branches have been created in the remote but none of the existing ones have been modified, then this method doesn't update the clone, and that means the new branches take up to 10 minutes to show up in repoman responses

Weird behavior, if it failed last time, we don't queue it again. But next time we do queue it (because we delete the failure cached value). So 2 consecutive almost concurrent calls to this end up updating the clone anyways. Why bother having this failure cache?

Maybe due to the retry policy of messages through Domino Messaging? The retry for this particular method is twice. Maybe this handles the edge case where the clone fails, but the message responses fails to send or receive, so the message is sent again, and we don't want to try the clone failing again.
not sure I follow. which messages? who would be the sender/receiver?
Remove this log, this is too much

The only time it's possible for a branch to not exist on the remote is if it has been deleted on the remote in the time when the clone was present in the clone cache. I think that the fileMetaDataCache (which the branches are taken from) is guaranteed to contain only the cloned branches. 

I don't believe that repoman ever directly modifies this local clone without modifying the upstream (like creating a new branch).
I was thinking of what happens if the branch is deleted in the remote, not through repoman I mean.
nit: change "repository" to "repo" to avoid a long line (URIs are long)
nit: change "repository" to "repo" to avoid a long line (URIs are long)

Nit: 
`
log.info(s"No stale branches in $repositoryKey. Local clone was not updated.")`
